name: What should be included in a window installation estimate?

mainEntity.name: What line items should be included in a proper window installation estimate so homeowners can compare bids fairly?
mainEntity.text: What line items should be included in a proper window installation estimate so homeowners can compare bids fairly?

acceptedAnswer.text: A useful estimate should clearly state the exact window product, quantity, sizes, installation method, and all labor involved. It should also show whether removal and disposal of old windows are included, along with flashing, sealants, insulation, trim repair, interior patching, and exterior finish work. If the project may uncover hidden damage, the estimate should explain how repair work will be priced. Homeowners should also look for warranty terms, lead times, permit handling, cleanup, and whether the quote includes tax or only base labor and materials. Comparing estimates line by line is better than comparing totals alone because one contractor may include essential steps that another treats as extras. In coastal homes, it is especially important to confirm water-management details like flashing and caulking. If any part of the scope is vague, ask for it in writing before signing.
acceptedAnswer.description: A complete estimate should list window type, size, quantity, labor, removal and disposal, flashing, trim, insulation, permits if needed, warranty terms, and any repair allowances. Missing line items often lead to change orders and higher final costs.
